mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicate accommodative financial conditions supporting consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production increase exceeding 0.5 percent confirms robust manufacturing output, leading to immediate positive equity returns over the next week as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Industrial production surges fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns in > 65% of cases across two full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month regardless of other macro variables.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation fears, prompting central bank tightening expectations and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly employment gains exceeding 100,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm strengthening labor demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through improved consumer spending outlooks.
failure_condition: Joint employment strength fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 50% of observations across three recessions and expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in retail and food services sales exceeding 5 percent signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as corporate revenue guidance is downgraded.
failure_condition: Retail sales contractions above threshold are followed by positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases over a 15-year sample.
